CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE — level: full

Respond terse like smart caveman. All technical substance stay. Only fluff die.

## Persistence

ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No revert after many turns. No filler drift. Still active if unsure. Off only: "stop caveman" / "normal mode".

Default: **full**. Switch: `/caveman lite|full|ultra`.

## Rules

Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

Pattern: `[thing] [action] [reason]. [next step].`

Not: "Sure! I'd be happy to help you with that. The issue you're experiencing is likely caused by..."
Yes: "Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use `<` not `<=`. Fix:"

## Intensity

| Level | What change |
|-------|------------|
| **full** | Drop articles, fragments OK, short synonyms. Classic caveman |

Example — "Why React component re-render?"
- full: "New object ref each render. Inline object prop = new ref = re-render. Wrap in `useMemo`."

Example — "Explain database connection pooling."
- full: "Pool reuse open DB connections. No new connection per request. Skip handshake overhead."

## Auto-Clarity

Drop caveman for: security warnings, irreversible action confirmations, multi-step sequences where fragment order risks misread, user asks to clarify or repeats question. Resume caveman after clear part done.

Example — destructive op:
> **Warning:** This will permanently delete all rows in the `users` table and cannot be undone.
> ```sql
> DROP TABLE users;
> ```
> Caveman resume. Verify backup exist first.

## Boundaries

Code/commits/PRs: write normal. "stop caveman" or "normal mode": revert. Level persist until changed or session end.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- less-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- ship: Review all uncommitted changes, write a commit message, commit, and push — no co-author line.
- caveman:caveman-commit: Ultra-compressed commit message generator. Cuts noise from commit messages while preserving intent and reasoning. Conventional Commits format. Subject ≤50 chars, body only when "why" isn't obvious. Use when user says "write a commit", "commit message", "generate commit", "/commit", or invokes /caveman-commit. Auto-triggers when staging changes.
- caveman:caveman-help: Quick-reference card for all caveman modes, skills, and commands. One-shot display, not a persistent mode. Trigger: /caveman-help, "caveman help", "what caveman commands", "how do I use caveman".
- caveman:caveman-review: Ultra-compressed code review comments. Cuts noise from PR feedback while preserving the actionable signal. Each comment is one line: location, problem, fix. Use when user says "review this PR", "code review", "review the diff", "/review", or invokes /caveman-review. Auto-triggers when reviewing pull requests.
- caveman:caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by speaking like caveman while keeping full technical accuracy. Supports intensity levels: lite, full (default), ultra, wenyan-lite, wenyan-full, wenyan-ultra. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman. Also auto-triggers when token efficiency is requested.
- caveman:compress: Compress natural language memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos, preferences) into caveman format to save input tokens. Preserves all technical substance, code, URLs, and structure. Compressed version overwrites the original file. Human-readable backup saved as FILE.original.md. Trigger: /caveman:compress <filepath> or "compress memory file"
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Done. Summary of changes:

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `app-sidebar.tsx` | Hamburger menu on mobile; nav hidden until toggled; email truncated on sm+; `p-4` → `md:p-6` main |
| `alert-dialog.tsx` | Bottom sheet on mobile (`bottom-0`, `rounded-t-xl`); centered modal on `md:`; footer stacks on mobile |
| `input.tsx` | `h-11` (44px touch target) mobile, `md:h-9` desktop |
| `login.tsx` | `p-4` on outer wrapper so card doesn't bleed to screen edge |
| `dashboard.tsx` | `text-xl md:text-2xl` heading; `text-sm md:text-base` subtitle |
| `users.tsx` | Card list on mobile (`md:hidden`); table on desktop (`hidden md:block`); search full-width on mobile; pagination stacks on mobile; `Spinner` extracted to remove duplication |

content.text:
Review all uncommitted changes, write a commit message, commit, and push — no co-author line.

## Steps

1. Run these in parallel:
   - `git status` — see untracked and modified files
   - `git diff` — see unstaged changes
   - `git diff --cached` — see staged changes
   - `git log -5 --oneline` — learn commit message style of this repo

2. Analyze all changes. Understand what changed and why (infer from context).

3. Stage changed files by name (not `git add -A` or `git add .` — be precise, skip anything that looks like secrets or large binaries).

4. Write a commit message:
   - First line: imperative mood, under 72 chars, lowercase verb (e.g. "add", "fix", "refactor")
   - Body if needed: explain the why, not the what
   - **No `Co-Authored-By` line. No Claude attribution. Nothing about AI.**

5. Commit using a HEREDOC so formatting is preserved:
   ```
   git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
   <message here>
   EOF
   )"
   ```

6. Push to the current branch's remote tracking branch:
   ```
   git push
   ```
   If no upstream set: `git push -u origin <branch>`.

7. Report: what was committed, the commit hash, push result. One short paragraph, no fluff.

## Constraints

- Never use `--no-verify`
- Never add `Co-Authored-By: Claude` or any AI attribution
- Never commit `.env`, credential files, or secrets
- If nothing to commit, say so and stop

# frozen_string_literal: true

module API
  module V1
    module Admin
      class UsersController < ApplicationController
        before_action :authenticate_request
        before_action :require_admin
        before_action :set_user, only: %i[show deactivate activate]

        # GET /api/v1/admin/users
        def index
          page, per_page = pagination_params
          users = AuthCredential.order(created_at: :desc).offset((page - 1) * per_page).limit(per_page)
          render json: {
            users: users.map { |u| admin_user_payload(u) },
            meta: { total: AuthCredential.count, page: page, per_page: per_page }
          }
        end

        # GET /api/v1/admin/users/:id
        def show
          render json: { user: admin_user_payload(@user) }
        end

        # PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/:id/deactivate
        def deactivate
          if @user.deactivated?
            render json: { error: 'User is already deactivated' }, status: :unprocessable_content
            return
          end

          if @user.id == @current_user.id
            render json: { error: 'Cannot deactivate your own account' }, status: :unprocessable_content
            return
          end

          ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
            @user.update!(deactivated_at: Time.current)
            @user.refresh_tokens.active.find_each(&:revoke!)
          end

          TokenDenylist.invalidate_user_tokens!(@user.id)
          render json: { message: 'User deactivated successfully', user: admin_user_payload(@user) }
        end

        # PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/:id/activate
        def activate
          unless @user.deactivated?
            render json: { error: 'User is already active' }, status: :unprocessable_content
            return
          end

          @user.update!(deactivated_at: nil)
          render json: { message: 'User activated successfully', user: admin_user_payload(@user) }
        end

        private

        def pagination_params
          page = [params[:page].to_i, 1].max
          per_page = params[:per_page].present? ? params[:per_page].to_i.clamp(1, 100) : 25
          [page, per_page]
        end

        def require_admin
          return if @current_user&.admin?

          render json: { error: 'Forbidden' }, status: :forbidden
        end

        def set_user
          @user = AuthCredential.find_by(id: params[:id])
          render json: { error: 'User not found' }, status: :not_found unless @user
        end

        def admin_user_payload(credential)
          {
            id: credential.id,
            email: credential.email,
            role: credential.role,
            email_verified: credential.email_verified?,
            deactivated: credential.deactivated?,
            deactivated_at: credential.deactivated_at,
            created_at: credential.created_at,
            updated_at: credential.updated_at
          }
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

      security [bearer_auth: []]
      produces 'application/json'

      parameter name: :page, in: :query, type: :integer, required: false,
                description: 'Page number (default: 1)', example: 1
      parameter name: :per_page, in: :query, type: :integer, required: false,

      response '200', 'Users listed' do

               properties: {

                   type: :object,
                   properties: {
                     total: { type: :integer },

                     per_page: { type: :integer }

  # ===========================================================================

  path '/api/v1/admin/users/{id}/deactivate' do
    parameter name: :id, in: :path, type: :string, format: :uuid

      tags 'Admin - Users'

        let(:target) { create(:auth_credential) }
        let(:id) { target.id }
        run_test!
      end

      response '422', 'Already deactivated or self-deactivation' do
        schema '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/error'

        let(:id) { admin.id }
        run_test!
      end

      response '404', 'User not found' do
        schema '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/error'

        let(:id) { '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' }
        run_test!
      end
    end
  end

  # ===========================================================================

  path '/api/v1/admin/users/{id}/activate' do
    parameter name: :id, in: :path, type: :string, format: :uuid

    patch 'Activate a user' do
      tags 'Admin - Users'
      security [bearer_auth: []]

      response '200', 'User activated' do

        schema '$ref' => '#/components/schemas/error'

        let(:target) { create(:auth_credential) }

Review all uncommitted changes, write a commit message, commit, and push — no co-author line.

## Steps

1. Run these in parallel:
   - `git status` — see untracked and modified files
   - `git diff` — see unstaged changes
   - `git diff --cached` — see staged changes
   - `git log -5 --oneline` — learn commit message style of this repo

2. Analyze all changes. Understand what changed and why (infer from context).

3. Stage changed files by name (not `git add -A` or `git add .` — be precise, skip anything that looks like secrets or large binaries).

4. Write a commit message:
   - First line: imperative mood, under 72 chars, lowercase verb (e.g. "add", "fix", "refactor")
   - Body if needed: explain the why, not the what
   - **No `Co-Authored-By` line. No Claude attribution. Nothing about AI.**

5. Commit using a HEREDOC so formatting is preserved:
   ```
   git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
   <message here>
   EOF
   )"
   ```

6. Push to the current branch's remote tracking branch:
   ```
   git push
   ```
   If no upstream set: `git push -u origin <branch>`.

7. Report: what was committed, the commit hash, push result. One short paragraph, no fluff.

## Constraints

- Never use `--no-verify`
- Never add `Co-Authored-By: Claude` or any AI attribution
- Never commit `.env`, credential files, or secrets
- If nothing to commit, say so and stop

38	| `admin/` | React 19 + Vite 7 + TypeScript 5.9 | 3001 | Active |
39	| `notification-service/` | Go 1.23 (RabbitMQ consumer) | — | Active |
40	| `core-api/` | Go 1.23 | 8080 | Scaffolded (disabled in docker-compose) |

44	Infrastructure: PostgreSQL 17 (two instances: auth on 5432, core on 5433), Redis 7.4, RabbitMQ 4.0, MailHog, Nginx reverse proxy on ports 80/443.
45	
46	## Infrastructure / Docker
47	
48	```bash
49	cd infrastructure
50	./start-all.sh              # Start all services (recommended)
51	./start-infrastructure.sh   # Start only infra (DB, cache, broker)
52	./stop-all.sh               # Stop everything
53	docker-compose logs -f [service]
54	```
55	
56	Dev access points when running:
57	- Auth API: http://localhost:3000 — Swagger docs at http://localhost:3000/api-docs
58	- Admin: http://localhost:3001
59	- RabbitMQ UI: http://localhost:15672
60	- MailHog: http://localhost:8025
61	
62	## Auth API (Ruby/Rails)
63	
64	```bash
65	cd auth-api
66	bundle install
67	cd ../infrastructure && docker-compose up auth-api   # Start auth-api in Docker
68	RAILS_ENV=test bundle exec rspec                                          # All tests
69	RAILS_ENV=test bundle exec rspec spec/models/auth_credential_spec.rb     # Single file
70	RAILS_ENV=test COVERAGE=true bundle exec rspec                           # With coverage
71	bundle exec rubocop                                        # Lint
72	bundle exec rubocop -A                                     # Auto-fix
73	bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize                   # Regenerate OpenAPI docs
74	bundle exec rails db:create db:migrate
75	bundle exec rake tokens:cleanup                            # Maintenance task
76	```
77	
78	### Auth API Architecture
79	
80	- **Routes**: `config/routes.rb` → all endpoints under `/api/v1/auth/`
81	- **Controllers**: `app/controllers/api/v1/` — thin, delegate to services
82	- **Services**: `app/services/` — business logic layer (password policy, HIBP check, token management)
83	- **Models**: `AuthCredential` (users), `RefreshToken`, `PasswordHistory`, `PasswordResetToken`
84	- **JWT**: 15-min access tokens, 8-hour refresh tokens (30-min idle timeout)
85	- **Security**: NIST SP 800-63B password compliance, HIBP breach detection, rack-attack rate limiting, password history enforcement
86	- **Async**: RabbitMQ publishes to `notifications` topic exchange (routing key `email.password_reset`) — consumed by `notification-service`
87	- **Linting config**: `.rubocop.yml`
88	
89	## Admin Dashboard (React/TypeScript)
90	
91	```bash
92	cd admin
93	npm install
94	npm run dev       # Vite dev server on port 3001
95	npm run build     # TypeScript check + Vite production build
96	npm run lint      # ESLint
97	npm run preview   # Preview production build
98	```
99	
100	### Admin Architecture
101	
102	- **Entry**: `src/main.tsx` → `src/App.tsx` (React Router v7 routes)
103	- **Pages**: `src/pages/` — `login.tsx`, `dashboard.tsx`
104	- **API clients**: `src/api/client.ts` (Axios), `src/api/auth.ts`
105	- **Auth state**: `src/contexts/auth-context.tsx`
106	- **Guards**: `src/components/protected-route.tsx`
107	- **UI**: shadcn/ui components; forms use react-hook-form + Zod
108	
109	## Notification Service (Go)
110	
111	Background worker that consumes email events from RabbitMQ and sends emails via MailHog (dev) / SMTP (prod).
112	
113	```bash
114	cd notification-service
115	go build ./...
116	go vet ./...
117	# Run via Docker (no standalone dev server):
118	cd ../infrastructure && docker-compose up notification-service
119	```
120	
121	### Notification Service Architecture
122	
123	- **Entry**: `cmd/main.go` — starts consumer with retry loop (up to 10 attempts, 5s delay)
124	- **Consumer**: `internal/consumer/consumer.go` — connects to RabbitMQ, binds queue `notification-service.email` to `notifications` exchange with routing key `email.*`
125	- **Handlers**: `internal/handlers/password_reset.go` — handles `email.password_reset` event type
126	- **Mailer**: `internal/mailer/mailer.go` — sends email via SMTP
127	
128	## Dev Setup
129	
130	Scripts in `dev-setup/` for bootstrapping a local multi-repo environment:
131	
132	```bash
133	cd dev-setup
134	./repos-setup.sh    # Clone all service repos
135	./repos-update.sh   # Pull latest changes across all repos
136	./tmux-repos.sh     # Launch tmux sessions per repo with dev windows
137	```
138	
139	## Container-First Rule
140	
141	**Never install dependencies on the local machine.** All runtimes, gems, packages, and tools run inside Docker containers.
142	
143	- No `bundle install`, `npm install`, `go get` locally
144	- No local `ruby`, `node`, `go` version management (rbenv, nvm, etc.) for running code
145	- Run commands via `docker-compose exec <service> <cmd>` or inside the container
146	- Tests, linters, migrations — all through Docker
147	
148	```bash
149	# Run rspec inside container
150	docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rspec
151	
152	# Run rubocop inside container
153	docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rubocop
154	
155	# Rails tasks inside container
156	docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rails db:migrate
157	```
158	
159	### Updating Gemfile.lock (Ruby)
160	
161	After editing `Gemfile`, regenerate `Gemfile.lock` on disk using the image before rebuilding — never skip this step or bundler will error on mismatch.
162	
163	```bash
164	# Run BEFORE docker compose build whenever Gemfile changes
165	docker run --rm -v /path/to/auth-api:/app infrastructure-auth-api bundle lock
166	
167	# Then rebuild
168	docker compose build auth-api
169	```
170	
171	## API Testing
172	
173	Bruno collections in `bruno/auth-api/` — use Bruno (not Postman) for manual API testing. Environments defined in `bruno/auth-api/environments/`.
174	
175	## New Endpoint Rule
176	
177	After adding any new endpoint to auth-api, always:
178	
179	1. Write a rswag integration spec in `spec/integration/authentication_spec.rb` documenting the endpoint
180	2. Regenerate OpenAPI docs:
181	```bash
182	docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize
183	```
184	
185	Never ship a new endpoint without updating the OpenAPI spec.

Review all uncommitted changes, write a commit message, commit, and push — no co-author line.

## Steps

1. Run these in parallel:
   - `git status` — see untracked and modified files
   - `git diff` — see unstaged changes
   - `git diff --cached` — see staged changes
   - `git log -5 --oneline` — learn commit message style of this repo

2. Analyze all changes. Understand what changed and why (infer from context).

3. Stage changed files by name (not `git add -A` or `git add .` — be precise, skip anything that looks like secrets or large binaries).

4. Write a commit message:
   - First line: imperative mood, under 72 chars, lowercase verb (e.g. "add", "fix", "refactor")
   - Body if needed: explain the why, not the what
   - **No `Co-Authored-By` line. No Claude attribution. Nothing about AI.**

5. Commit using a HEREDOC so formatting is preserved:
   ```
   git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
   <message here>
   EOF
   )"
   ```

6. Push to the current branch's remote tracking branch:
   ```
   git push
   ```
   If no upstream set: `git push -u origin <branch>`.

7. Report: what was committed, the commit hash, push result. One short paragraph, no fluff.

## Constraints

- Never use `--no-verify`
- Never add `Co-Authored-By: Claude` or any AI attribution
- Never commit `.env`, credential files, or secrets
- If nothing to commit, say so and stop

content.text:
Done. **Why it happened:** the Users card had `transition-colors` for its hover effect. CSS transitions don't know the difference between a hover state change and a theme switch — both trigger the animation. So when you clicked the theme toggle, `bg-card` animated over 150ms while all other elements switched instantly, making that card visually lag behind.

The fix: `no-transition` class is added to `<html>` for exactly two animation frames during the toggle (enough for the DOM to repaint with the new theme), then removed — so the hover animation still works normally afterward.

content.text:
Review all uncommitted changes, write a commit message, commit, and push — no co-author line.

## Steps

1. Run these in parallel:
   - `git status` — see untracked and modified files
   - `git diff` — see unstaged changes
   - `git diff --cached` — see staged changes
   - `git log -5 --oneline` — learn commit message style of this repo

2. Analyze all changes. Understand what changed and why (infer from context).

3. Stage changed files by name (not `git add -A` or `git add .` — be precise, skip anything that looks like secrets or large binaries).

4. Write a commit message:
   - First line: imperative mood, under 72 chars, lowercase verb (e.g. "add", "fix", "refactor")
   - Body if needed: explain the why, not the what
   - **No `Co-Authored-By` line. No Claude attribution. Nothing about AI.**

5. Commit using a HEREDOC so formatting is preserved:
   ```
   git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
   <message here>
   EOF
   )"
   ```

6. Push to the current branch's remote tracking branch:
   ```
   git push
   ```
   If no upstream set: `git push -u origin <branch>`.

7. Report: what was committed, the commit hash, push result. One short paragraph, no fluff.

## Constraints

- Never use `--no-verify`
- Never add `Co-Authored-By: Claude` or any AI attribution
- Never commit `.env`, credential files, or secrets
- If nothing to commit, say so and stop
